skill.name: database-migrations-migration-observability
skill.description: Migration monitoring, CDC, and observability infrastructure
skill.summary: Enterprise-grade observability infrastructure for database migrations with CDC pipelines and real-time alerting
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 24 patterns but all are false positives. The skill uses standard monitoring libraries (prometheus-client, kafka-python, requests) for legitimate observability purposes. Network calls target infrastructure APIs (Kafka Connect, Grafana, Slack webhooks) as expected for monitoring. No shell execution, cryptographic operations, or suspicious patterns found. Minor risk due to network access for webhook integrations.

low_findings.title: HTTP requests to external services
low_findings.description: Skill makes HTTP requests to Kafka Connect API, Grafana API, and Slack webhooks for monitoring purposes. This is expected behavior for observability infrastructure but requires API credentials.

content.user_title: Build migration observability and CDC pipelines
content.value_statement: Database migrations lack visibility into progress, errors, and data consistency. This skill provides enterprise-grade observability with real-time metrics, anomaly detection, and automated alerting.
actual_capabilities: Create observable MongoDB migrations with metrics collection and structured logging
Set up Change Data Capture pipelines using Debezium and Kafka for real-time data sync
Configure Prometheus metrics for migration duration, throughput, and error tracking
Implement anomaly detection for low throughput and high error rates
Automate Grafana dashboard creation for migration monitoring
Integrate CI/CD pipelines with migration health checks and alerting
limitations: Requires external infrastructure (Kafka, Prometheus, Grafana) to be pre-configured
Network access needed for Kafka Connect, Grafana API, and Slack webhook integrations
Supports MongoDB, PostgreSQL - additional databases require custom connector configuration
Does not provide automatic rollback - manual intervention required for failed migrations

use_cases.title: Zero-downtime production migrations
use_cases.description: Monitor large-scale database migrations in real-time with automated alerting when data lag exceeds thresholds or error rates spike.

use_cases.title: CDC pipeline implementation
use_cases.description: Set up Change Data Capture from PostgreSQL to target systems with Debezium, tracking replication lag and event throughput.
use_cases.target_user: Data platform engineers building real-time sync

use_cases.title: Migration dashboard automation
use_cases.description: Generate Grafana dashboards programmatically showing migration progress, data lag, and error rates with threshold-based alerting.

prompt_templates.title: Basic migration monitoring setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an observable MongoDB migration script with Prometheus metrics for duration and document count. Include structured logging with winston.
prompt_templates.scenario: Setting up basic observability for a simple migration

prompt_templates.title: CDC pipeline with Debezium
prompt_templates.prompt: Set up a Debezium CDC connector for PostgreSQL with Kafka. Configure metrics for events processed, consumer lag, and replication lag.
prompt_templates.scenario: Building real-time data synchronization between databases

prompt_templates.title: Anomaly detection and alerting
prompt_templates.prompt: Implement anomaly detection for migration throughput and error rates. Send alerts to Slack when throughput drops below 50% of expected or error rate exceeds 1%.
prompt_templates.scenario: Proactive monitoring with automated incident notification

prompt_templates.title: Full observability stack
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a complete migration observability solution with Prometheus metrics, Grafana dashboard automation, Slack alerting, and CI/CD integration for health checks.
prompt_templates.scenario: Enterprise-grade migration monitoring for production deployments

output_examples.input: Set up MongoDB migration with metrics
output_examples.output: Observable migration class with Prometheus histograms for duration, counters for documents processed, and winston logging to file and console. Metrics endpoint exposed for Prometheus scraping.
Kafka Connect connector configuration with pgoutput plugin, heartbeat intervals, and metrics for consumer lag and replication lag. Events streamed to database.changes topic.
Grafana dashboard with panels for migration rate (rows per second), data lag in seconds with color thresholds, and error rate over time. Auto-provisioned via Grafana API.

best_practices: Always use transactions with session-based execution for migration atomicity and rollback support
Set heartbeat intervals (10 seconds) in CDC connectors to detect replication stalls quickly
Configure multiple alerting channels (Slack, email) with severity-based routing for critical vs warning alerts
anti_patterns: Do not skip metrics registration - unobservable migrations cannot be monitored for anomalies
Avoid polling intervals longer than 30 seconds for progress tracking - delays anomaly detection
Never send credentials in dashboard configurations - use environment variables or secrets management

faq.question: What databases are supported for CDC?
faq.answer: PostgreSQL is fully supported via pgoutput plugin. MongoDB, MySQL, and SQL Server require additional Debezium connector configuration.

faq.question: How does anomaly detection work?
faq.answer: The system monitors rows per second and error rates. Alerts trigger when throughput drops below 50% of expected or error rate exceeds 1%.

faq.question: Can I use this without Kafka?
faq.answer: Basic migration observability works without Kafka. CDC pipelines require Kafka for event streaming.

faq.question: How are Grafana dashboards created?
faq.answer: Dashboards are auto-provisioned via the Grafana API using bearer token authentication. Dashboard JSON is generated programmatically.

faq.question: What metrics are collected by default?
faq.answer: Migration duration, documents/rows processed, error counts, consumer lag, and replication lag in seconds.

faq.question: How do I integrate with CI/CD?
faq.answer: Use the provided GitHub Actions workflow template with health check steps that verify migration completion and data lag thresholds.
